title: Antidepressant response convergence vs binding breadth (Cipriani 2018)
source: Cipriani et al., Lancet 2018 (response OR vs placebo)
interpretation: Across 11 antidepressants spanning SSRI/SNRI/TCA/atypical, response ORs vs placebo span 1.49-2.13 with coefficient of variation 0.10 (tight clustering -- the empirical '~60% convergence' phenomenon). Binding breadth (1 to 5 targets) is essentially UNcorrelated with response: Pearson r=0.39 (p=0.23), Spearman r=0.38. This SUPPORTS the framework's qualitative claim -- molecular target multiplicity does not predict clinical efficacy; different 'apertures' reach comparable outcomes. CAVEAT: this is a correlational convergence in published meta-analytic ORs, consistent with MANY explanations (regression to common endpoint, trial design, ceiling effects); it does NOT uniquely confirm the aperture/regime mechanism. Defensible claim: response convergence is real and breadth-independent; the aperture taxonomy is one interpretation, not a validated cause.
